questionHtml:
The owner of the generator is suspicious of the switch manufacturer’s claims, so they look back through the past 200 occasions when the switches were replaced. The records show whether no switches, one switch or two switches had failed.

The data the owner collected are shown in the following table.

Show that the expected value of no switches failing in the generator, during the last 200 occasions when the switches were replaced, is 126.
markschemeHtml:
P(no fail)=0.63          A1

multiplying by 200          M1

=126          AG

 

Note: Award A0M0 for a flawed approach to find P(no fail)=0.63, e.g. 126200=0.63, which is reverse engineering.

[2 marks]

meta.title: Show that the expected value of no switches failing in the generator, during the last 200 occasions when the switches were replaced, is 126.
